what_it_does: Stylesheet for the Studio's Imagery subsystem: the image library (tabs, toolbar/tag bar, image grid, cards, empty state), Projects/Hubs layouts, a 360 panorama viewer with hotspot pins, the AI Studio (v3 'deck-restraint' redesign with banner, tabs, presets, pipelines, prompt/source/compose/params/runbar/results/history/chat panes), a mask editor, and a modal image editor (crop overlay, filters, ratios, rotate, AI-edit panel).
observations: All values reference design tokens (var(--paper-*), --ink-*, --accent-gold*, --font-*, --r-*, --s-*, --shadow-*, --status-*); almost no raw colors except a handful of rgba(31,26,18,...) overlays/backdrops.
Class prefixes segment the file: cv-im-* (imagery), cv-pano-* (360 viewer), cv-as-* (AI Studio v3), cv-mask-* (mask editor), cv-ed-* (image editor modal).
Header comment marks a 'v3 — deck-restraint redesign' block, implying an earlier AI Studio styling (cv-im-ai-*) is also still present in the same file.
Several keyframe animations defined inline: cv-pano-pulse, cv-as-pulse, cv-ed-fade.
Uses color-mix(in oklch, ...) for one pill background.
A media query at min-width 1200px switches the results grid to two columns based on :nth-child(5) of a resizable column.
Repeating-linear-gradient used for skeleton/running-pipeline striping and the editor stage checkerboard.
standouts: Two AI-studio styling eras coexist (cv-im-ai-* legacy banner/grid AND the cv-as-* v3 deck-restraint set).
The 360 panorama viewer styling (cv-pano-*) with pulsing hotspot pins and labels.
A full modal image editor (cv-ed-*) with crop box handles, rule-of-thirds grid overlay, filter thumbnails, aspect-ratio buttons, and an AI-edit results grid.
conventions: Follows the project's token-reference discipline (var(--token) everywhere, off-token literals limited to translucent dark overlays).
Uses the warm-paper/gold-ink palette described in its own header comment.
BEM-ish hyphenated class naming under cv- prefixes, grouped by subsystem with banner comments.
concerns: Raw rgba(31,26,18,...) and rgba(224,192,16,...) and rgba(251,247,236,...) literals recur for overlays/striping rather than tokenized alpha variants.
Mix of two variable naming families (--paper-2/--ink-3 vs --bg-surface/--fg-primary/--fg-muted) within one file suggests two token vocabularies in play.
The .cv-as-results-grid two-column rule keys off :nth-child(5) of a generic .cv-resizable-col, a positional coupling that is brittle if column order changes.
notes: Large multi-subsystem stylesheet for Studio's imagery/AI-studio/mask/image-editor surfaces; token-driven, carries a documented v3 redesign block alongside legacy AI-studio styles.
questions: Are the legacy cv-im-ai-* AI Studio styles still consumed, or superseded by the cv-as-* v3 set?
Why do two token families (--paper-*/--ink-* and --bg-*/--fg-*) both appear — are they aliases or distinct layers?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the header states 'Imagery subsystem + Settings + Editor + 360 viewer' and the file delivers exactly those plus the AI Studio and mask editor.
apparent_themes: Token-referenced single-source styling (no copies of values)
Subsystem-segmented stylesheet for image-centric Studio surfaces
intention_signals: Aims to style the full image-management + AI-generation + editing workflow of the Studio entirely from the shared token system, keeping a documented redesign trail (v3 deck-restraint) in place.
novelty: The combination in one stylesheet of a 360 pano viewer, a brush mask editor, and a modal crop/filter/AI image editor — a complete imaging toolchain styled from one token set.
contribution: Contributes the visual layer for the Studio's imagery, AI image-generation studio, panorama/hotspot viewer, mask editor, and modal image editor surfaces.
summary_for_embedding: A token-driven CSS stylesheet styling the ConceptV Studio's imagery library, AI Studio (v3 deck-restraint), 360 panorama viewer, mask editor, and modal image editor.
intention_for_embedding: To style the Studio's complete image management, AI-generation, and editing workflow entirely from the shared design-token system.
